name: Enlistment Office
description: 1. When the number of injured Troops exceeds the capacity limit of the Infirmary, the Enlistment Office will recruit some reserves to replace some of the Troops you've lost in battle. 2. Recruit from the reserve using Loyalty, it will start to accumulate again after using the reserve. 3. Factors affecting the rate: The lower the Loyalty is, the faster it accumulate. The fewer the severely injured in the Infirmary, the faster it accumulates. The more reserves (excluding reserves that can be enlisted immediately) in the Enlistment Office, the faster it accumulates. Please note, the Loyalty will stop increasing after reaching the limit. 4. You can obtain Loyalty by collecting rewards from daily missions or gathering if the reserve in the Enlistment Office is not empty. You will get more as your Furnace level increases. 5. The capacity of Enlistment Office is 4 times the Infirmary capacity.
html:
Description

1. When the number of injured Troops exceeds the capacity limit of the Infirmary, the Enlistment Office will recruit some reserves to replace some of the Troops you've lost in battle.
2. Recruit from the reserve using Loyalty, it will start to accumulate again after using the reserve.
3. Factors affecting the rate: The lower the Loyalty is, the faster it accumulate. The fewer the severely injured in the Infirmary, the faster it accumulates. The more reserves (excluding reserves that can be enlisted immediately) in the Enlistment Office, the faster it accumulates. Please note, the Loyalty will stop increasing after reaching the limit.
4. You can obtain Loyalty by collecting rewards from daily missions or gathering if the reserve in the Enlistment Office is not empty. You will get more as your Furnace level increases.
5. The capacity of Enlistment Office is 4 times the Infirmary capacity.

name: Marksman Camp
description: The building is used to train and upgrade Marksman. Chiefs can unlock the building at Furnace Lv.8. They upgrade to the level of your Furnace and are usually one of the upgrade requirements needed to upgrade your Furnace to the next level. Each upgrade increases the Training Capacity(how many troops you can train at one time), reduces training time, and boosts your power. At certain upgrade levels, you will gain access to higher tier troops. Once you have unlocked T5 troops, you will gain the ability to promote your lower tier troops to the highest trainable tier. Researching the technology Camp Expansion in the Growth tab of the Research Center will further increase the number of troops you can train at one time. Researching Training Tools, also in the Growth tab, decreases the time needed to train a group of troops.

name: Furnace
description: Just a big, and probably dangerous, bonfire at the beginning. This is your town HQ and the first construct you open. Its level determines both the max level you can upgrade other buildings to along with which buildings you can open up and when you open them up. Upgrading it will increase the amount of heat it produces and also increases your power. The Furnace has a secondary function that is equally as important: keeping your Survivors warm during the day, night, and during snowstorms. This helps prevent your Survivors from becoming sick. When sick, your Survivors will not be able to work until they are well again.
html:

Just a big, and probably dangerous, bonfire at the beginning. This is your town HQ and the first construct you open. Its level determines both the max level you can upgrade other buildings to along with which buildings you can open up and when you open them up. The Furnace has a secondary function that is equally as important: keeping your Survivors warm during the day, night, and during snowstorms. This helps prevent your Survivors from becoming sick. When sick, your Survivors will not be able to work until they are well again.

name: Shelter
description: These buildings house your Survivors giving them a place to rest, relax, and sleep. As of this writing, a maximum of eight Shelters can be built. As you upgrade the Shelters and furniture inside them, the amount of Survivors that can join your town increases up to a maximum of 32. Upgrading the Shelters increases Survivor Sleep Quality, Comfort, Mood, and Warmth along with a boost in power. You will need to max out the upgrades of certain furniture inside the building before being able to upgrade the building to the next level. Keeping these buildings and the furniture inside them upgraded as much as you can is important for the health and happiness of your Survivors. Until you get your Clinic built, your Survivors will come back here if they get sick.

name: Cookhouse
description: Second building you open. Pretty self-explanatory. This is where your Survivors go to eat. Though not its only source, the Cookhouse uses a certain amount of meat from the Hunter's Hut. Keep this in mind in the early game and make sure your Hunter's Hut is producing the maximum amount of meat it can. This building is a main component in keeping your Survivors healthy and happy. If they aren't getting the proper nutrition, their mood can drop and they will even get sick. This happened to me. Even though I was being good with setting the Furnace to max during the night and storms and my Shelters were all upgraded to their max, my Survivors started getting sick. Took me a minute, but I eventually discovered it was because my food quality was too low. Luckily, there is a menu you can change to increase the quality of food for your Survivors. When you tap on your Cookhouse, at the bottom you will see a little yellow menu button.
html:
Description

Second building you open. Pretty self-explanatory. This is where your Survivors go to eat. Though not its only source, the Cookhouse uses a certain amount of meat from the Hunter's Hut. Keep this in mind in the early game and make sure your Hunter's Hut is producing the maximum amount of meat it can.

This building is a main component in keeping your Survivors healthy and happy. If they aren't getting the proper nutrition, their mood can drop and they will even get sick. This happened to me. Even though I was being good with setting the Furnace to max during the night and storms and my Shelters were all upgraded to their max, my Survivors started getting sick. Took me a minute, but I eventually discovered it was because my food quality was too low.

Luckily, there is a menu you can change to increase the quality of food for your Survivors. When you tap on your Cookhouse, at the bottom you will see a little yellow menu button.

name: Dawn Academy
description: As the server progresses to a certain stage (with fire crystal level5 era), the Expert System will be unlocked. When the Furnace reaches level 25, the new building [Dawn Academy] and the Tundra Trek gameplay will become available. Unlocking experts allows them to be stationed at the Dawn Academy, where their professional skills will help boost your town’s development and inject new vitality into it. Begin exploration journeys to visit experts from various fields these experts are individuals with profound knowledge or exceptional craftsmanship. The knowledge they impart will greatly benefit your town’s growth. Every day at 00:00 UTC, 20 units of free Trek Supplies will automatically recover a must have for avid explorers in the Tundra. Additional 10 units Trek Supplies can be collected at 8:00 UTC and 16:00 UTC from the Dawn Academy. During your journeys, you’ll encounter various random events and have a chance to meet experts. Each time you meet an expert, help them resolve their troubles to build friendship with them. Once befriended, you can invite them to settle in your town. Giving them gifts can increase their Affinity, making them more trusting and willing to share more of their knowledge and skills. Experts who settle in your town will hold lectures at the Dawn Academy, teaching the townspeople and soldiers their respective expertise. Each expert has their own specialty, and the skills they impart differ. Their expertise will bring great help to your town’s development and your expeditions in the Tundra.
html:
Description

As the server progresses to a certain stage (with fire crystal level5 era), the Expert System will be unlocked. When the Furnace reaches level 25, the new building [Dawn Academy] and the Tundra Trek gameplay will become available. Unlocking experts allows them to be stationed at the Dawn Academy, where their professional skills will help boost your town’s development and inject new vitality into it.

Begin exploration journeys to visit experts from various fields these experts are individuals with profound knowledge or exceptional craftsmanship. The knowledge they impart will greatly benefit your town’s growth.
Every day at 00:00 UTC, 20 units of free Trek Supplies will automatically recover a must have for avid explorers in the Tundra. Additional 10 units Trek Supplies can be collected at 8:00 UTC and 16:00 UTC from the Dawn Academy.

During your journeys, you’ll encounter various random events and have a chance to meet experts. Each time you meet an expert, help them resolve their troubles to build friendship with them. Once befriended, you can invite them to settle in your town.
Giving them gifts can increase their Affinity, making them more trusting and willing to share more of their knowledge and skills.

Experts who settle in your town will hold lectures at the Dawn Academy, teaching the townspeople and soldiers their respective expertise.
Each expert has their own specialty, and the skills they impart differ. Their expertise will bring great help to your town’s development and your expeditions in the Tundra.

name: Beast Cage
description: 1. This is a lovely Beast Cage where the beasts captured from the Wilderness are kept. (Only one of each type of beast can be captured.) They will play and grow with you. 2. As you gain more Taming XP and your city develops, the variety of beasts that can be captured will become more diverse. 3. After taming the captured beasts, they will become your pets and loyal companions. The stronger a beast, the harder it is to tame it. 4. You can upgrade your tamed pets, help them to advance, and enhance their battle skills or stats through refinement. 5. When a pet reaches Lv. 10 and completes advancement, it will unlock a special Talent Skill. Use it wisely to boost you in battles on the tundra. 6. Tamed pets can be dispatched to the Wilderness for treasure hunts. After each treasure hunt, a pet will bring back a special Personal Treasure and Ally Treasure that can be shared.
html:
Description

1. This is a lovely Beast Cage where the beasts captured from the Wilderness are kept. (Only one of each type of beast can be captured.) They will play and grow with you.
2. As you gain more Taming XP and your city develops, the variety of beasts that can be captured will become more diverse.
3. After taming the captured beasts, they will become your pets and loyal companions. The stronger a beast, the harder it is to tame it. 4. You can upgrade your tamed pets, help them to advance, and enhance their battle skills or stats through refinement.
5. When a pet reaches Lv. 10 and completes advancement, it will unlock a special Talent Skill. Use it wisely to boost you in battles on the tundra.
6. Tamed pets can be dispatched to the Wilderness for treasure hunts. After each
treasure hunt, a pet will bring back a special
Personal Treasure and Ally Treasure that can be shared.

name: Lighthouse
description: In this building, you will find Intelligence missions you can complete. These consist of rescuing Survivors, hunting beasts, or having your Heroes battle their way through an investigation of a building. You can upgrade the Searchlight by completing the required amount of missions shown at the bottom left of the screen. It will upgrade to a maximum of level 20. Upgrading the Searchlight allows for more missions to be found during refreshes along with better quality missions. Better quality missions reward you with better prizes. Once you have completed all the missions available, you will need to wait until the reset to do more. Always make sure to complete these as much as you can. The prizes are more than worth it.
html:
Description

In this building, you will find Intelligence missions you can complete. These consist of rescuing Survivors, hunting beasts, or having your Heroes battle their way through an investigation of a building.

You can upgrade the Searchlight by completing the required amount of missions shown at the bottom left of the screen. It will upgrade to a maximum of level 20.

Upgrading the Searchlight allows for more missions to be found during refreshes along with better quality missions. Better quality missions reward you with better prizes. Once you have completed all the missions available, you will need to wait until the reset to do more.

Always make sure to complete these as much as you can. The prizes are more than worth it.

name: Arena
description: This is where you will go to test your PVP mettle against other Chief's. Bring your strongest Heroes and climb the Ranks. Challenging and winning against other Chiefs awards you Arena points. You will not lose points if you lose. Nor will you gain or lose points by winning or losing if another Chief challenges you. To gain Arena points, YOU must initiate the challenge. Rewards for your performance in the Arena are handed out daily and weekly, with the Arena seasons being reset every week. Winning in the Arena also gets you Arena Tokens which can be used in the Arena Store.
html:
Description

This is where you will go to test your PVP mettle against other Chief's. Bring your strongest Heroes and climb the Ranks.

Challenging and winning against other Chiefs awards you Arena points. You will not lose points if you lose. Nor will you gain or lose points by winning or losing if another Chief challenges you. To gain Arena points, YOU must initiate the challenge.

Rewards for your performance in the Arena are handed out daily and weekly, with the Arena seasons being reset every week.

Winning in the Arena also gets you Arena Tokens which can be used in the Arena Store.

name: Chief’s House
html:
Description

The Chief's House contains Edicts that can be activated to grant different benefits to your town. Each Edict costs Contentment points « ». These points are earned when your Survivors are working or can be purchased with Diamonds.




Urgent Mobilization

Force survivors at all worksites to work for 48 hours (Survivor Time), -10 Mood for all Survivors.



Cooldown: 8h (Real Time)

Cost: 50,000




Rush Job

Gives 5 days' worth of resources from all worksites (Survivor Time).



Cooldown: 24h (Real Time)

Cost: 150,000




Comprehensive Care

Heals all sick Survivors in the City.



Cooldown: 4h (Real Time)

Cost: 150,000




Double Time

Accelerates new building construction or new upgrades in the City by 20% starting in the next 5 minutes.



Cooldown: 23h (Real Time)

Cost: 800,000




Productivity Day

+100% Output across all resource worksites for 24 hours (Survivor Time), -10 Mood for all Survivors.



Cooldown: 12h (Real Time)

Cost: 50,000




Festivities

+50 Mood and +30 Comfort during a Day of City festivities (Survivor Time).



Cooldown: 24h (Real Time)

Cost: 50,000





💡 Tip: Combining Urgent Mobilization with Productivity Day is an excellent strategy to quickly boost your resource supply. Just remember to activate Festivities afterward to restore your Survivors’ Mood and Comfort.

name: Suggestion Box
description: The Suggestion Box is split between two tabs: Suggestions and SOS. Suggestions Paying attention to the suggestions gives you an idea of what your Survivors are happy about, or not so happy about. If a Survivor isn't happy about something, their mood drops and your Public Opinion Rating(POR) will drop. So making sure you take care of the unhappy suggestions is very important. SOS Every now and then you will be called upon to make a decision to settle an issue in your town. Depending on the choices you make to resolve the issue, your POR will either rise or fall. Do your best to keep your POR high to keep your Survivors happy and to get the best daily rewards.
html:
Description

The Suggestion Box is split between two tabs: Suggestions and SOS.

Suggestions

Paying attention to the suggestions gives you an idea of what your Survivors are happy about, or not so happy about. If a Survivor isn't happy about something, their mood drops and your Public Opinion Rating(POR) will drop. So making sure you take care of the unhappy suggestions is very important.

 

SOS

Every now and then you will be called upon to make a decision to settle an issue in your town. Depending on the choices you make to resolve the issue, your POR will either rise or fall.

 

Do your best to keep your POR high to keep your Survivors happy and to get the best daily rewards.

name: Explorers Cabin
description: If you've reached your maximum amount of offline Idle time, a chest will appear above this that you can directly receive your Idle rewards from. Otherwise, tapping this will take you to Exploration mode.

If you've reached your maximum amount of offline Idle time, a chest will appear above this that you can directly receive your Idle rewards from. Otherwise, tapping this will take you to Exploration mode.

You can read more about super refinements here

name: Fire Crystal Marksman Camp
description: This building is used to train and upgrade Fire Crystal Marksman. Chiefs can unlock the building when their Furnace have reached Fire Crystal level. They upgrade to the level of your Furnace and are usually one of the upgrade requirements needed to upgrade your Furnace to the next level. Each upgrade increases the Training Capacity(how many troops you can train at one time), reduces training time, and boosts your power. At certain upgrade levels, you will gain access to higher tier troops. Once you have unlocked T5 troops, you will gain the ability to promote your lower tier troops to the highest trainable tier. Researching the technology Camp Expansion in the Growth tab of the Research Center will further increase the number of troops you can train at one time. Researching Training Tools, also in the Growth tab, decreases the time needed to train a group of troops.
html:
Description

This building is used to train and upgrade Fire Crystal Marksman. Chiefs can unlock the building when their Furnace have reached Fire Crystal level.

They upgrade to the level of your Furnace and are usually one of the upgrade requirements needed to upgrade your Furnace to the next level. Each upgrade increases the Training Capacity(how many troops you can train at one time), reduces training time, and boosts your power.

At certain upgrade levels, you will gain access to higher tier troops. Once you have unlocked T5 troops, you will gain the ability to promote your lower tier troops to the highest trainable tier.

Researching the technology Camp Expansion in the Growth tab of the Research Center will further increase the number of troops you can train at one time. Researching Training Tools, also in the Growth tab, decreases the time needed to train a group of troops.

Requirements

name: Fire Crystal Embassy
description: This is one of your Alliance buildings. The purpose of it is to have a place to keep reinforcements that are sent to you by Alliance members. It is also what dictates the amount of help you can get from your Alliance members when it comes to Construction, Research, and Healing. Upgrading this building is typically a requirement to upgrade your Furnace to the next level. Upgrading the Embassy increases the total amount of reinforcements your Alliance can send you, the amount of times your Alliance members can help you speed up Construction/Research/Healing, and boosts your power.
html:
Description

This is one of your Alliance buildings. The purpose of it is to have a place to keep reinforcements that are sent to you by Alliance members. It is also what dictates the amount of help you can get from your Alliance members when it comes to Construction, Research, and Healing.

Upgrading this building is typically a requirement to upgrade your Furnace to the next level. Upgrading the Embassy increases the total amount of reinforcements your Alliance can send you, the amount of times your Alliance members can help you speed up Construction/Research/Healing, and boosts your power.

Requirements

ATTENTION

name: Fire Crystal Infirmary
description: This is where your injured soldiers go to heal. Upgrading this building increases the Infirmary capacity along with giving you a boost in power. Keep the Infirmary at the same level of your HQ to ensure the maximum capacity. This is important because if your Infirmary fills up, your troops will start to die in battle. Upgrading the Ward Expansion technology in the Growth tab of the Research Center increases the capacity of your Infirmary, too. Researching Bandaging, also in the Growth tab, decreases healing time.
html:
Description

This is where your injured soldiers go to heal. Upgrading this building increases the Infirmary capacity along with giving you a boost in power.

Keep the Infirmary at the same level of your HQ to ensure the maximum capacity. This is important because if your Infirmary fills up, your troops will start to die in battle.

Upgrading the Ward Expansion technology in the Growth tab of the Research Center increases the capacity of your Infirmary, too. Researching Bandaging, also in the Growth tab, decreases healing time.

Requirements

ATTENTION

description: Just a big, and probably dangerous, bonfire at the beginning. This is your town HQ and the first construct you open. Its level determines both the max level you can upgrade other buildings to along with which buildings you can open up and when you open them up. Upgrading it will increase the amount of heat it produces and also increases your power. The Furnace has a secondary function that is equally as important: Keeping your Survivors warm during the day, night, and during snowstorms. This helps prevent your Survivors from becoming sick. When sick, your Survivors will not be able to work until they are well again.
html:
Description

Just a big, and probably dangerous, bonfire at the beginning. This is your town HQ and the first construct you open. Its level determines both the max level you can upgrade other buildings to along with which buildings you can open up and when you open them up. Upgrading it will increase the amount of heat it produces and also increases your power.

The Furnace has a secondary function that is equally as important:
Keeping your Survivors warm during the day, night, and during snowstorms.
This helps prevent your Survivors from becoming sick. When sick, your Survivors will not be able to work until they are well again.

Requirements

ATTENTION

The construction times recorded beneath are the base times. It does not take into account any reduction benefits like State Buff, Research, Zinman's skill and etc. For most players, the time to build would be lesser than what is listed here.
